source: UBS/Credit Suisse Global Wealth Report 2023 (data year 2022) global adult wealth distribution — approximate anchor points read off the report's pyramid/distribution figures, in USD at market exchange rates. Not an official percentile table: the curve between anchors is our own log-linear interpolation, built only to give a reader a rough sense of where a given net worth sits worldwide.
note: netWorth is the lower bound of net worth (USD) at which an adult reaches the given global percentile. The first anchor (netWorth 0) is a floor: zero or negative net worth is treated as flat at the 10th percentile rather than interpolated, since a log scale has no value for zero or below. Above the last anchor ($5,000,000), position is clamped and reported as 'top 0.1%' rather than interpolated further.
